Where the points come from

pullsTeam ATeam B  ·  ⚡ eligible team-up  ·  team shape
pick some heroes…

Each whisker is the 95% bootstrap interval around a hero's value. A dimmed number means the interval still touches zero, so the sign is not settled.

▶Methods & data

Each value is an adjusted plus-minus: a ridge logistic regression of who wins on who was on each side, controlling for both full rosters, team-ups, role composition, map, mode, and the individual players. It measures a hero's contribution to win log-odds, not a raw win rate. Scale: 1 point ≈ 2.5% win chance in a close game.

The model

One row per match, with yi = 1 when side A wins. Each hero enters as a signed presence, +1 on your side, −1 on the enemy, 0 if absent or mirrored:

xih = 1{h main on A} − 1{h main on B} ∈ {−1, 0, +1}

Win probability is logistic in a sum of effects:

Pr(yi = 1) = σ(ηi), σ(z) = 1 / (1 + e−z)

ηi = β0
  + Σh βh xih   heroes
  + Σt τt zit   team-ups
  + Σp αp uip   players
  + Σc ωc vic   composition
  + Σm γm mapim + Σk δk modeik   map · mode

What's held constant

  • Both teams' heroes. A pick is graded against games where the enemy runs it too, not just yours.
  • Team-ups. τt is the bonus beyond the heroes that form the combo, counted when the combo is eligible (both heroes mained on one side).
  • Player skill. A term αp for every player with ≥2 games, so a hero isn't credited for its smurf.
  • Role composition. Signed 2-2-2 / 1-3-2 categories, counted only when both sides field a clean structure.
  • Map, mode, and a leak-free pre-match rank gap, so venue and lobby skill can't leak in.

Points to win chance

A value is relative log-odds, centered on the average hero, so a pick is really a swap: fielding hero h over hero j moves the score by βh − βj. The logistic link σ turns that score into the win % on the scoreboard, and it is steepest near an even game, so one point moves the needle most in a coin flip. Drag below to see it.

η = log-odds  →  win chance

The fit is ridge-penalized, so correlated or noisy picks get pulled toward average unless the data insists, and it is tuned out-of-sample. Every value ships with a 1,999-draw bootstrap interval; the roster shows it as a whisker, and a shape shown with a faint ≈ is one whose interval still touches zero.
